steps.title: Use the graph-choice shortcut
steps.content: Compare the intercepts of the answer choices with the line in the question. The correct equation must have the same $x$- and $y$-intercepts as the shown graph.

steps.title: Read the intercepts
steps.content: The shown line crosses the $x$-axis at $(3,0)$ and the $y$-axis at $(0,-5)$. The matching choice, $5x-3y=15$, has those same intercepts: setting $y=0$ gives $x=3$, and setting $x=0$ gives $y=-5$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other equations
steps.content: A choice with the signs reversed, so its intercepts are $(-3,0)$ and $(0,5)$. The other choices swap the intercept sizes because the coefficients of $x$ and $y$ are reversed. Only the matching choice matches the graph.
